CONTENT.Club career: Born in Gillingham, he attended The Howard School in Rainham, Kent. Binks is a childhood Gillingham-fan and his mother Jo worked in the club's ticket office. Binks joined the Tottenham Hotspur academy at the age of six in 2007.On 18 February 2020, Binks left Tottenham Hotspur and signed a professional contract with Montreal Impact of Major League Soccer. He made his debut for the club on 26 February 2020 against Deportivo Saprissa during a CONCACAF Champions League Round of 16 tie. He started and played the whole match as Montreal drew 0–0 but advanced on a 2–0 aggregate. Binks made his MLS debut on the 29th of that month, playing the whole game in a 2–1 victory over the New England Revolution.On 13 August 2020, Binks was transferred to Serie A club Bologna, with him staying on loan with the Impact until the end of the 2020 MLS season. Binks made his debut on 26 September 2021 in a Serie A away match to Empoli which Bologna lost 4–2.On 18 July 2022, Binks was loaned to Como for a season.On 28 July 2023, he was loaned to EFL Championship side Coventry City.On 19 June 2024, Binks made his loan transfer to Coventry City permanent for an undisclosed fee.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played on 4 May 2024
CONTENT.International career: Binks is eligible for England through birth and Scotland through his maternal grandfather being from Dunbar. He represented Scotland at under-16 in the Victory Shield between 2016 and 2017.

SUMMARY: Luis Thomas Binks (born 2 September 2001) is an English professional footballer who plays as a defender for Coventry City. Born in England, he has represented both England and Scotland internationally at age group level.
